key_insight: Polarization-resolved third-harmonic measurements reveal hidden, oppositely signed chiral responses in a 2D halide perovskite, enabling strong telecom-band circularly polarized light detection with giant third-order nonlinear circular dichroism.
engineering_relevance: This work points to compact telecom-wavelength photonic devices for CPL sensing, optical information processing, encryption, and anti-counterfeiting using chiral perovskite nonlinear optics.
method: Measured third-harmonic generation circular dichroism (THG-CD) in chiral 2D perovskite (R/S-MBACl)2PbI4.
Performed polarization-resolved THG detection to separate orthogonal emission channels.
Compared total-THG vs channel-resolved signals to expose hidden chiral anisotropy.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports dissymmetry factors exceeding 1.9 when hidden polarization-resolved contributions are separated, while conventional total-THG analysis underestimates the effect due to cancellation.
watch_item: Device performance may appear weaker if evaluated only by total THG intensity, so polarization-resolved readout is essential for reliable implementation.
paper_title: Telecom-band Chiral Light Detection through Hidden Giant Third-Order Nonlinear Circular Dichroism in Two-dimensional Halide Perovskite
